dpr_reports.title: Core HTTP Client Delegation to urllib3

assumption_checks.assumption: `urllib3` is a robust, well-tested, and actively maintained library suitable for `Requests`'s needs.
assumption_checks.reason: No explicit evidence of decay yet, but the risk stems from `urllib3` being an external dependency. Potential evidence would include: new critical security vulnerabilities discovered in `urllib3`, a significant decrease in `urllib3`'s maintenance activity (e.g., slow bug fixes, infrequent releases), or reports of `urllib3` failing stability tests under high load.

assumption_checks.assumption: The performance and feature set provided by `urllib3` are sufficient for the diverse use cases of `Requests`.
assumption_checks.reason: No explicit evidence of decay yet. Potential evidence would include: `Requests` users reporting performance regressions traced back to `urllib3` updates, or a significant portion of the user base requesting advanced HTTP features (e.g., HTTP/3, QUIC support, more granular connection controls) that `urllib3` does not provide or has no roadmap for.

dpr_reports.recommendation: MONITOR: Core HTTP Client Delegation to urllib3 shows early warning signs. Track metrics and prepare contingency plan.

dpr_reports.title: Introduction of a Session Object for State Persistence

assumption_checks.assumption: A significant portion of `Requests` users require stateful HTTP interactions, such as maintaining authentication or persistent configurations.
assumption_checks.reason: No decay signals detected in current monitoring cycle

assumption_checks.assumption: The overhead of managing `Session` objects (e.g., memory, initialization) is acceptable given the convenience and functionality it provides.
assumption_checks.reason: No decay signals detected in current monitoring cycle

dpr_reports.recommendation: STABLE: Introduction of a Session Object for State Persistence assumptions appear to hold. Continue routine monitoring.

dpr_reports.title: Standardized CA Certificate Verification via certifi

assumption_checks.assumption: `certifi` provides a reliable, widely trusted, and regularly updated certificate bundle that meets the security needs of most users.
assumption_checks.reason: No explicit evidence of decay yet. Potential evidence would include: `certifi` releases falling significantly behind major CA bundle updates, reports of `certifi` bundles containing outdated, missing, or revoked certificates, leading to widespread SSL/TLS connectivity errors for `Requests` users.

assumption_checks.assumption: The benefits of a standardized, current CA bundle outweigh the slight increase in dependency footprint.
assumption_checks.reason: No decay signals detected in current monitoring cycle

dpr_reports.recommendation: MONITOR: Standardized CA Certificate Verification via certifi shows early warning signs. Track metrics and prepare contingency plan.

dpr_reports.title: Explicit Python 3.10+ Requirement

assumption_checks.assumption: The majority of the user base is on, or can reasonably migrate to, Python 3.10 or newer.
assumption_checks.reason: No decay signals detected in current monitoring cycle

assumption_checks.assumption: The benefits of leveraging modern Python language features (e.g., structural pattern matching, `TypeAlias`, improved `typing` features) and standard library enhancements outweigh the cost of dropping older version support.
assumption_checks.reason: No decay signals detected in current monitoring cycle

dpr_reports.recommendation: STABLE: Explicit Python 3.10+ Requirement assumptions appear to hold. Continue routine monitoring.

dpr_reports.title: Character Encoding Detection Preference (charset_normalizer)

assumption_checks.assumption: `charset_normalizer` provides superior or equivalent performance and accuracy to `chardet` for diverse web content.
assumption_checks.reason: No explicit evidence of decay yet. Potential evidence would include: a noticeable increase in user reports of garbled text (`r.text`) or `JSONDecodeError` when `charset_normalizer` is used, or the emergence of a new, widely adopted encoding detection library that demonstrably outperforms `charset_normalizer` in accuracy or speed across common web content.

assumption_checks.assumption: Providing robust automatic character encoding detection is crucial for a user-friendly HTTP library that handles various web servers.
assumption_checks.reason: No decay signals detected in current monitoring cycle

dpr_reports.recommendation: MONITOR: Character Encoding Detection Preference (charset_normalizer) shows early warning signs. Track metrics and prepare contingency plan.
